channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Rachel Morin's 'bum' killer mooched off locals before murdering mom of 5: lawyer
item.description: Rachel Morin's family attorney says the Maryland woman's migrant killer was a "bum" who raped and murdered the mother of five and should not have been in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Artisans in Guatemala hand-stitch velvet cloaks for Holy Week processions
item.description: SAN MIGUEL ESCOBAR, Guatemala (AP) — Stitch by stitch, artisans have worked for months on the elaborate garments that will debut this week in Holy Week processions across Guatemala and beyond.
item.dc:creator: SONIA PÉREZ D., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tennessee’s GOP leads the fight to deny public education to children without documents
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Damian Felipe Jimenez has many dreams about his future — he could be a restaurant owner, a scientist or maybe something else.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italy's Meloni will test her mettle as EU-US bridge when she meets Trump in Washington
item.description: MILAN (AP) — Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni will test her mettle as a bridge between the EU and the United States when she meets with U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: San Francisco's new mayor is starting to unite the fractured city
item.description: Democrats nationally may be in turmoil, but liberals in San Francisco are hopeful over a new mayor's collaborative style in a city known for its infighting and chaotic streets.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har And Olga R. Rodriguez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOGE targets a community service program in its latest cost-cutting effort
item.description: A 30-year-old community service program for young adults that operates across the U.S. has become the latest target of the Trump administration’s campaign to slash government spending.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine's Kherson kill 1 person and wound 9
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian glide bombs and artillery struck a city in southern Ukraine on Wednesday, killing one person and wounding nine others as Moscow forces continued daily attacks across the country.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Body of missing Washington state grandmother found buried under shed partially encased in concrete
item.description: Marcia Norman, 82, was found buried in concrete below a shed her handyman, who police say was the last person to see her alive, built a day after her disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration plans to end free tax filing program
item.description: But, in mid-March, the IRS staff assigned to the program were told to stop working on it for the 2026 tax filing season, the two sources told the AP.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Former FAA contractor pleads guilty to spying for Iran, sharing private info on US airports, energy industry
item.description: A naturalized U.S. citizen working as an FAA contractor pleaded guilty this week to spying in the U.S. on behalf of Iran, while providing them with information on U.S. airports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mississippi River named 'most endangered' by conservation group, cites FEMA cuts
item.description: The organization said the federal government plays a key role in protecting the river and helping homeowners prepare for, and rebuild after, major flooding.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, ABC 4

item.title: 2 service members dead after vehicle crashes near New Mexico Border Patrol station
item.description: The third service member involved in the accident was still hospitalized in critical condition as of Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died of complications from diabetes, says NYC medical examiner
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Actor Michelle Trachtenberg died as a result of complications from diabetes, New York City's medical examiner said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White House invites Rachel Morin's mother to press briefing to share daughter's story
item.description: The mother of a Maryland woman who was killed by an individual who entered the country illegally addressed reporters at the White House on Wednesday as the Trump administration seeks to go on offense over the case of an individual who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Some may have another chance to see the northern lights Wednesday
item.description: The SWPC is forecasting an increase in geomagnetic activity starting around 9 p.m. UTC, or 4 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Temu and Shein say they're raising prices due to tariffs
item.description: Both companies said they would be making “price adjustments” starting April 25, although neither provided details about the size of the increases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to rewrite part of the Endangered Species Act that prohibits harming the habitats of endangered and threatened species.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Webber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawyers file wrongful death notice against Idaho city for family of boy shot by police
item.description: A California law firm notified the city of Pocatello, Idaho, on Wednesday of its intention to file a federal wrongful death lawsuit on the behalf of the family of Victor Perez, a 17-year-old boy with intellectual disabilities who was shot multiple times by four police officers.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-NYPD officer sentenced to 18 months after conviction for helping China stalk an expat
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former New York police sergeant was sentenced Wednesday to 18 months in prison in a U.S. case about China's pursuit of critics abroad, a sentence that came after two members of Congress urged the judge to spare him from time behind bars.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas DOGE calls for sanctions, audit of insurance company accused of spying
item.description: DOGE Lawmakers and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton have accused Superior of hiring private investigators to get information on lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, NewsNation

item.title: I-35 crash lawsuits: Amazon denies allegations, bond hearing set
item.description: Weldekeal-Araya was working for a third-party carrier hauling a load for Amazon when police said he crashed into nearly stopped traffic in a construction zone.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, NewsNation

item.title: Colorado company to become first to refuel Space Force asset
item.description: Astroscale U.S. Inc., a Denver-based on-orbit services and logistics company, will perform two refueling operations of a U.S. Department of Defense satellite.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, NewsNation

item.title: California sues to stop Trump from imposing sweeping tariffs
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom sued the Trump administration on Wednesday, challenging the president's authority to impose sweeping tariffs that have set off a global trade war.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman sues McAlister's Deli, claims tooth found in meal
item.description: A Myrtle Beach restaurant is being sued for negligence after a woman claims she cracked her molar on a tooth that was in her meal.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Family says ICE agents smashed car window in seizing Guatemalan man who's seeking asylum
item.description: NEW BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A Massachusetts family is demanding answers from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, complaining its agents smashed a car window with a hammer and detained a man who they claim had applied for asylum.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Walgreens class action drug settlement: Thursday deadline for $100M
item.description: The lawsuit alleges Walgreens sold generic drugs at a higher cost to customers with insurance than to Prescription Savings Club members, according to court docs.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 3 dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US measles cases surpass 700 with outbreaks in six states. Here's what to know
item.description: Kansas health officials confirmed five new cases of measles Wednesday in an outbreak in the southwest corner of the state that's linked to Texas and New Mexico. Last week, U.S. measles cases topped 700 as Indiana joined five others states with active outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Boasberg moves to hold Trump administration in contempt over deportation flights
item.description: A federal judge found probable cause Wednesday to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt for willfully disobeying his order to immediately halt deportations under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act and turn around any airborne planes. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: Newsom says California will sue over Trump tariffs
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom and Attorney General Rob Bonta announced plans to sue the Trump administration over recent tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: US charges leaders of 'brutal Mexican cartel,' offers $8M reward for brothers' capture
item.description: The U.S. is offering an $8 million reward for the capture of two brothers accused of running a "brutal Mexican cartel" that officials say is "poisoning Americans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP Was There: A truck bomb rips through a federal building in Oklahoma City in 1995
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — EDITORS NOTE: On April 19, 1995, a former U.S. Army soldier parked a rented Ryder truck loaded with a powerful bomb made of fertilizer and fuel oil outside a federal office building in Oklahoma City. The blast at the Alfred P.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's a look at recent aircraft tragedies, mishaps and close calls
item.description: The crash of a small plane in New York over the weekend killed all six on board — a physician couple and their accomplished children and partners — during a family trip to the Catskills.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
